scenario: An on-call engineer at Riverstone Bank inspects 950 Camunda 8.8 loan-application instances stuck on an incident caused by a misconfigured downstream API. The API has been decommissioned and the affected instances cannot be salvaged; the team needs to clear them from the active list without retrying them so the next 24 hours of work are unblocked.
question: Which selection-and-operation sequence in Operate is appropriate for clearing these unsalvageable instances?

options.text: Build a selection of the affected process instances by checking the boxes, then apply a batch cancellation operation; confirm and monitor progress in the Operations panel
Build a selection of the affected process instances by checking the boxes, then apply a batch retry operation; the broker will then prune any instance whose downstream API has been removed
Delete the deployed process definition from the deployments view; Operate then cascades a cancellation to every running instance of that definition automatically
Apply a batch retry operation across the entire process definition without building a selection; Operate scopes the retry only to instances currently in an incident state

a.text: Correct. When stuck instances cannot be salvaged, the documented pattern is to check the boxes to build a selection, pick the batch cancellation operation, confirm it, and follow progress in the Operations panel.

b.text: Incorrect. A batch retry tries to resume execution; it does not prune unsalvageable instances. To remove them, the operator applies a batch cancellation operation on the selection instead.

c.text: Incorrect. Operate does not document a cascading cancellation triggered by deleting a process definition; cancellations are applied through selections and the cancel operation on the instance list view.

d.text: Incorrect. Operations are applied to a selection the operator builds by checking the boxes; there is no documented option to run a retry across an entire definition while implicitly scoping it to incidents.

explanation: Operate models bulk work as selections plus operations. To remove unsalvageable instances, the operator builds a selection of the affected instances, picks the batch cancellation operation, confirms it, and watches the Operations panel for progress.
